articles.headline: Government Unveils PM GatiShakti 2.0 with Focus on Green Infrastructure and Logistics Efficiency
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government announced an advanced phase of the PM GatiShakti National Master Plan, termed GatiShakti 2.0, emphasizing sustainable development and green infrastructure.
• This new phase aims to further integrate planning and execution across 16 ministries for multi-modal connectivity and logistics efficiency, incorporating environmental sustainability.
• Key focus areas include developing green highways, promoting electric vehicle charging infrastructure along corridors, and integrating renewable energy projects into logistics hubs.
• The initiative seeks to leverage digital technologies like AI and machine learning for predictive analytics in project management and reduce carbon footprint in the logistics sector.
• Expected outcomes include significant cost reduction for businesses, faster project implementation, and enhanced environmental stewardship through sustainable practices.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment Models
Environmental Impact Assessment
prelimsPoints: PM GatiShakti launched in October 2021.
It integrates 16 ministries for infrastructure development.
GatiShakti 2.0 focuses on green infrastructure and digital integration.
Aims to reduce logistics costs and enhance efficiency.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of integrated infrastructure planning for India's economic growth and global competitiveness.
Analyze how the 'green' component of GatiShakti 2.0 contributes to India's sustainable development goals and climate commitments.
Evaluate the potential challenges and opportunities in implementing a large-scale, digitally-driven infrastructure project like GatiShakti 2.0.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration depicting integrated infrastructure projects like roads, railways, and ports with green energy elements.
articles.trivia: PM GatiShakti aims to cut logistics costs from 13-14% of GDP to 8% by 2030, aligning with global benchmarks.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Delivers Landmark Ruling on Parliamentary Privilege vs. Fundamental Rights
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a crucial judgment clarifying the scope of parliamentary privileges in relation to the fundamental rights of citizens, particularly freedom of speech and expression.
• The ruling addressed a long-standing debate on the balance between safeguarding legislative proceedings and upholding individual liberties.
• The court emphasized that while parliamentary privileges are essential for the effective functioning of the legislature, they are not absolute and must be exercised in consonance with constitutional provisions.
• The judgment is expected to set a precedent for future cases involving conflicts between legislative authority and fundamental rights, impacting media reporting and public discourse.
• Legal experts highlight the ruling's importance in strengthening democratic accountability and ensuring that legislative actions remain subject to judicial review within defined parameters.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Article 105 deals with powers, privileges, etc., of the Houses of Parliament and of the members and committees thereof.
Article 19 guarantees freedom of speech and expression.
The ruling seeks to balance legislative autonomy with individual rights.
Parliamentary privileges are not codified in their entirety.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the delicate balance between parliamentary privileges and fundamental rights in a democratic setup, with reference to the recent Supreme Court judgment.
Discuss the implications of this ruling on the freedom of press and public discourse regarding legislative functioning.
Examine the historical context and evolution of parliamentary privileges in India and how judicial interventions have shaped their interpretation.
articles.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: The concept of parliamentary privilege is largely derived from British parliamentary traditions and is enshrined in the Indian Constitution.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Powers, Privileges and Immunities of Parliament and its Members

articles.headline: India Champions Global South Priorities at UN General Assembly, Calls for Equitable Climate Finance
articles.explainer:
• India's External Affairs Minister addressed the United Nations General Assembly (UNGA), reiterating India's commitment to championing the voice of the Global South.
• The address focused on critical issues such as equitable access to climate finance, reform of multilateral institutions, and sustainable development goals (SDGs).
• India called for developed nations to fulfill their climate finance commitments and stressed the need for technology transfer to developing countries.
• The minister highlighted India's own contributions to global initiatives, including renewable energy projects and disaster resilience infrastructure, as a model for South-South cooperation.
• The statement underscored India's growing role as a leading advocate for a more inclusive and representative global order.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora—their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: UNGA is the main deliberative, policymaking and representative organ of the UN.
India is a strong advocate for Global South issues.

Climate finance refers to local, national or transnational financing—drawn from public, private and alternative sources of financing—that seeks to support mitigation and adaptation actions that will address climate change.
SDGs were adopted by all UN member states in 2015.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's evolving role as a leader of the Global South and its implications for international relations and multilateral institution reforms.
Analyze the challenges in achieving equitable climate finance and technology transfer, and critically evaluate India's proposals at the UNGA.
Examine how India's foreign policy prioritizes sustainable development and addresses global challenges like climate change and food security.
articles.imageDescription: Image of the UN General Assembly hall with country delegates.
articles.trivia: India has been a member of the United Nations since its founding in 1945.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Concerns and Global Volatility
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly meeting, deciding to maintain the repo rate at its current level.
• The decision was influenced by persistent concerns over elevated core inflation and the need to ensure price stability, despite moderating headline inflation.
• The MPC also highlighted global economic uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions and volatile commodity prices, as key factors impacting India's economic outlook.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to supporting economic growth while remaining vigilant against inflationary pressures, maintaining an 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• Analysts suggest that future rate decisions will heavily depend on the trajectory of monsoon, global oil prices, and government fiscal measures.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
MPC consists of six members (3 from RBI, 3 appointed by Government).
Primary objective of MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth in the current global and domestic economic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflationary pressures in India.
Examine the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance of the RBI and its implications for credit growth and investment in the Indian economy.
articles.imageDescription: Image of the Reserve Bank of India headquarters in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: New National Policy on Sustainable Urban Waste Management Launched to Boost Circular Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched a comprehensive National Policy on Sustainable Urban Waste Management, aiming for a circular economy approach.
• The policy focuses on source segregation, waste-to-wealth initiatives, promoting advanced recycling technologies, and reducing landfill dependency.
• It mandates greater involvement of urban local bodies (ULBs) and private sector players in waste collection, processing, and disposal, with emphasis on Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR).
• Capacity building for ULB officials and awareness campaigns for citizens on waste reduction and segregation are integral parts of the new framework.
• The policy is aligned with India's Swachh Bharat Mission and aims to achieve significant improvements in urban sanitation and environmental quality by 2030.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: The policy aims to reduce landfill dependency and promote recycling.
It emphasizes source segregation of waste.
Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is a key component.
Aligned with Swachh Bharat Mission goals.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the potential of a circular economy approach in addressing India's urban waste management challenges.
Discuss the role of technology and public-private partnerships in implementing the new National Policy on Sustainable Urban Waste Management.
Analyze the socio-economic and environmental benefits of effective waste management and the challenges in achieving behavioral change among citizens.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating the waste management cycle from segregation to recycling and waste-to-energy.
articles.trivia: India generates over 1.5 lakh metric tonnes of municipal solid waste every day.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful test of its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology demonstrator.
• This critical test involved controlled landing and precise navigation maneuvers, simulating future operational flights.
• The RLV program aims to significantly reduce the cost of space access by enabling repeated use of launch vehicles, a key goal for future space missions.
• The technology is expected to pave the way for India's own space shuttle-like capabilities, enhancing its competitive edge in the global space market.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing fully reusable rockets, which are crucial for ambitious future missions like human spaceflight and satellite mega-constellations.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV program aims to reduce space launch costs.
The test involved controlled landing and navigation.
RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) is part of this program.
India is developing its own reusable rocket technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its global competitiveness.
Analyze the economic and scientific benefits of reducing the cost of space access through technologies like RLV.
Examine the challenges and future prospects for India in developing fully reusable rocket systems and their impact on future space missions.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle prototype during re-entry.
articles.trivia: ISRO first successfully tested the RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) in 2016, performing an atmospheric re-entry experiment.

articles.headline: New National Health Mission Initiative Targets Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in Urban Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched a new initiative under the National Health Mission (NHM) specifically designed to combat Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in urban settings.
• The program focuses on early detection, screening, and management of common NCDs like diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular diseases, and certain cancers.
• It emphasizes lifestyle modifications, public awareness campaigns, and strengthening primary healthcare infrastructure in urban areas to deliver comprehensive NCD care.
• Digital health solutions and community health workers will play a crucial role in reaching vulnerable populations and ensuring continuity of care.
• This initiative is a response to the growing burden of NCDs, which are a leading cause of morbidity and mortality in India, particularly in urban centers.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: NCDs include diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular diseases, and cancers.
The initiative is under the National Health Mission (NHM).
Focuses on early detection, screening, and management.
Aims to strengthen urban primary healthcare infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rising burden of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in India, especially in urban areas, and its socio-economic implications.
Discuss the strategies and challenges involved in implementing a comprehensive NCD control program under the National Health Mission.
Suggest innovative approaches and public-private partnerships that can enhance the effectiveness of NCD prevention and management in India.
articles.imageDescription: Infographic showing common NCDs and preventive measures.
articles.trivia: NCDs account for over 60% of all deaths in India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government Policies and Interventions for Health Sector

articles.headline: Government Initiates Comprehensive Review of India's Cyber Security Framework Amidst Rising Threats
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has initiated a comprehensive review of India's existing cyber security framework.
• The move comes in response to a surge in sophisticated cyberattacks targeting critical infrastructure, government networks, and financial institutions.
• The review aims to strengthen existing laws, update national strategies, and enhance coordination among various agencies, including CERT-In.
• Emphasis will be placed on developing indigenous cyber security capabilities, fostering public-private partnerships, and building a skilled workforce.
• The updated framework is expected to address emerging threats like AI-driven cyberattacks and supply chain vulnerabilities, ensuring a robust digital ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
prelimsPoints: MeitY is reviewing India's cyber security framework.
CERT-In is the national agency for responding to cyber security incidents.
The review addresses sophisticated cyber threats and critical infrastructure protection.
Focus on indigenous capabilities and public-private partnerships.
mainsPoints: Examine the evolving landscape of cyber threats in India and the challenges in protecting critical national infrastructure.
Discuss the key components of a robust national cyber security strategy and how the proposed review aims to strengthen India's digital defenses.
Analyze the role of international cooperation and indigenous capability building in enhancing India's cyber security posture.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract image representing cyber security with digital locks and network lines.
articles.trivia: The National Cyber Security Policy of India was introduced in 2013.

articles.headline: India and Japan Hold High-Level Talks on Enhancing Defence Cooperation and Bilateral Trade
articles.explainer:
• India and Japan conducted their annual strategic dialogue, focusing on strengthening defence cooperation, regional security, and boosting bilateral trade and investment.
• Discussions included joint military exercises, technology transfer in defence manufacturing, and cooperation in emerging security domains like cyber and space.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and discussed strategies to counter challenges to regional stability.
• Economic agenda covered diversification of supply chains, green energy investments, and collaboration in critical and emerging technologies.
• The talks underscored the growing convergence of strategic interests between India and Japan, key partners in the Quad framework.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are strategic partners and members of the Quad.
Discussions covered defence, trade, and regional security.
Both nations advocate for a free and open Indo-Pacific.
Focus on critical and emerging technologies and green energy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-Japan strategic partnership in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how enhanced defence cooperation and economic collaboration between India and Japan contribute to their respective national interests and regional stability.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India and Japan in diversifying supply chains and fostering innovation in critical technologies.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed during a bilateral meeting.
articles.trivia: India and Japan established a 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership' in 2014.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Committee Discusses Uniform Civil Code (UCC) with Legal Experts and Stakeholders
articles.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee held extensive consultations on the Uniform Civil Code (UCC), inviting views from various legal experts, religious organizations, and civil society groups.
• The discussions focused on the constitutional aspects of UCC, its potential impact on personal laws, and the challenges in achieving consensus across diverse communities.
• Committee members explored different models for UCC implementation, including a phased approach or specific legislative reforms rather than a wholesale overhaul of personal laws.
• Stakeholders presented arguments both for and against the UCC, highlighting concerns about cultural diversity and gender justice implications.
• The committee's report is expected to provide recommendations on the feasibility and framework for a potential Uniform Civil Code in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Article 44 of the Indian Constitution directs the state to endeavor to secure a UCC.
UCC aims to replace diverse personal laws with a common set of laws governing marriage, divorce, inheritance, etc.
Parliamentary committees play a crucial role in legislative scrutiny.
Goa is the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code.
mainsPoints: Examine the constitutional mandate and socio-political implications of implementing a Uniform Civil Code in India.
Discuss the arguments for and against the UCC, considering issues of cultural diversity, gender justice, and religious freedom.
Analyze the challenges in building consensus for a UCC and suggest potential approaches for its phased implementation, drawing lessons from international experiences.
articles.imageDescription: Image of the Indian Parliament building.
articles.trivia: The Supreme Court has, on several occasions, urged the government to implement a Uniform Civil Code.

articles.headline: Government Boosts Agricultural Exports and Value Chain Development with New Policy Measures
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare announced new policy measures aimed at significantly boosting India's agricultural exports and strengthening value chains.
• The initiatives include enhanced support for farmer producer organizations (FPOs), investment in post-harvest infrastructure, and promotion of 'Brand India' for agricultural products.
• Focus areas are high-value agricultural commodities, organic products, and processed foods, targeting new international markets.
• The policy emphasizes technology adoption, quality control, and adherence to international phytosanitary standards to enhance competitiveness.
• This move is expected to increase farmers' income, reduce post-harvest losses, and position India as a major global agricultural exporter.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
articles.source: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
prelimsPoints: New policies aim to boost agricultural exports.
Support for FPOs and post-harvest infrastructure are key.
Focus on high-value and organic agricultural products.
Aims to increase farmer income and reduce losses.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of India's agricultural sector to become a global export powerhouse and the policy interventions required to achieve this.
Analyze how strengthening agricultural value chains and promoting FPOs can enhance farmer income and reduce post-harvest losses.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in ensuring adherence to international quality and phytosanitary standards for Indian agricultural exports.
articles.imageDescription: Image of diverse Indian agricultural produce ready for export.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest producers of several agricultural commodities, including milk, pulses, and spices.

articles.headline: New Initiatives Launched for Conservation of Endangered Species and Their Habitats
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change unveiled a series of new initiatives aimed at strengthening the conservation efforts for several endangered species across India.
• The focus is on critical habitats, establishing more eco-sensitive zones, and implementing community-based conservation models.
• Projects include enhanced protection for specific species like the Great Indian Bustard, Snow Leopard, and various freshwater turtle species.
• The initiatives incorporate advanced technologies like drone surveillance, genetic monitoring, and satellite tracking for effective wildlife management.
• These efforts align with India's commitments under the Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD) and aim to protect the nation's rich biodiversity.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Initiatives target endangered species like Great Indian Bustard, Snow Leopard.
Focus on critical habitats and eco-sensitive zones.
Utilizes drone surveillance and genetic monitoring.
Aligned with India's CBD commitments.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major threats to biodiversity in India and evaluate the effectiveness of current conservation strategies.
Analyze the role of community participation and technological advancements in strengthening wildlife protection and habitat conservation.
Examine India's international commitments for biodiversity conservation and how national initiatives contribute to achieving these goals.
articles.imageDescription: Picture of a Great Indian Bustard in its natural habitat.
articles.trivia: India is home to 4 of the 36 global biodiversity hotspots.

articles.headline: Government Announces New Policy to Foster Deep-Tech Startups and Innovation Ecosystem
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Science & Technology unveiled a new national policy aimed at accelerating the growth of deep-tech startups in India.
• The policy provides financial incentives, easier access to research infrastructure, and streamlined regulatory processes for startups working on cutting-edge technologies.
• Focus areas include Artificial Intelligence (AI), Quantum Computing, Biotechnology, Advanced Materials, and Space Technology.
• It emphasizes collaboration between academia, industry, and government to create a robust innovation ecosystem and attract venture capital.
• The initiative seeks to position India as a global hub for deep-tech innovation, driving economic growth and creating high-skilled jobs.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: New policy targets deep-tech startups.
Provides financial incentives and infrastructure access.
Focuses on AI, Quantum Computing, Biotechnology, etc.
Aims to foster collaboration between academia, industry, government.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of deep-tech startups for India's economic growth, national security, and global competitiveness.
Analyze the challenges faced by deep-tech startups in India and how the new policy aims to address these issues.
Examine the role of government, academia, and private sector in building a thriving deep-tech innovation ecosystem in India.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract image representing advanced technology and innovation with glowing circuits.
articles.trivia: India ranks third globally in terms of the number of startups, with over 100 unicorns.

articles.headline: National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Report Highlights Progress in Skill Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a progress report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, focusing on skill development initiatives.
• The report highlighted significant strides in integrating vocational education into mainstream schooling and higher education curricula.
• It showcased the establishment of new skill hubs, industry partnerships for internships, and the introduction of credit-based vocational courses.
• The policy aims to equip students with future-ready skills, enhance employability, and bridge the skill gap in various sectors.
• Challenges remain in scaling vocational training infrastructure and ensuring equitable access across rural and urban areas.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 seeks to integrate vocational education.
Progress reported in establishing skill hubs and industry partnerships.
Aims to equip students with future-ready skills.
Challenges include scaling infrastructure and equitable access.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the progress and challenges in implementing the skill development objectives of the National Education Policy 2020.
Discuss how vocational education can be effectively integrated into India's mainstream education system to enhance youth employability and address the skill gap.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to quality skill development programs, particularly for students in rural and marginalized communities.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in vocational training activities.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history after those of 1968 and 1986.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government Policies and Interventions for Education Sector

articles.headline: Election Commission Explores Digital Voting Pilot Projects and Other Electoral Reforms
articles.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) announced its intention to explore pilot projects for digital voting, particularly for absentee voters and specific demographics.
• The discussions on electoral reforms also included proposals for greater transparency in campaign financing, regulation of social media during elections, and strengthening the Model Code of Conduct.
• ECI emphasized ensuring the security, integrity, and verifiability of any digital voting system to maintain public trust.
• The move is aimed at enhancing voter participation and making the electoral process more accessible and efficient.
• Stakeholder consultations are underway to gather feedback from political parties, technology experts, and civil society organizations on these proposed reforms.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
prelimsPoints: ECI exploring digital voting pilot projects.
Aims to enhance voter participation and accessibility.
Reforms also include campaign finance and social media regulation.
Integrity and security of digital voting are key concerns.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential benefits and challenges of introducing digital voting in India, particularly for absentee voters and NRIs.
Analyze the significance of electoral reforms in strengthening democratic processes and ensuring free and fair elections in India.
Examine the role of the Election Commission of India in upholding the integrity of elections and adapting to new technological and social challenges.
articles.imageDescription: Symbolic image of a ballot box with digital elements, representing electoral modernization.
articles.trivia: The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body established under Article 324 of the Constitution.

articles.headline: India's Manufacturing PMI Hits Record High, Signaling Robust Economic Recovery
articles.explainer:
• India's Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) reached a record high in September, indicating strong expansion in the manufacturing sector.
• The surge was driven by a robust increase in new orders, higher output, and improved business confidence, reflecting healthy domestic demand.
• Employment in the manufacturing sector also saw a notable rise, suggesting job creation and positive sentiment among businesses.
• Despite some input cost pressures, firms remained optimistic about future production, with export orders also contributing to the growth.
• This sustained growth in manufacturing is a key indicator of India's broader economic recovery and resilience amidst global headwinds.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Industrial policy and its effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: PMI is an indicator of economic trends in the manufacturing and service sectors.
A PMI reading above 50 indicates expansion.
Record high PMI signifies strong growth in manufacturing.
Driven by new orders, output, and employment growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the robust growth in India's manufacturing PMI and its implications for the overall economic recovery.
Discuss the role of government policies, such as 'Make in India' and PLI schemes, in boosting the manufacturing sector.
Examine the challenges that persist for India's manufacturing sector despite positive indicators, such as global supply chain disruptions and competition.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating an upward trend in economic indicators with factory silhouettes.
articles.trivia: The Services PMI and Composite PMI are other key indicators tracking economic activity.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Growth and Development of Indian Economy

articles.headline: India Hosts Regional Summit on Enhancing Maritime Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India hosted a high-level regional summit focused on strengthening maritime security cooperation among littoral states in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• The summit addressed challenges such as piracy, maritime terrorism, illegal fishing, and drug trafficking, emphasizing a collaborative approach.
• Discussions included proposals for joint patrols, intelligence sharing mechanisms, and capacity building for coastal nations.
• India reiterated its vision for SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) and its commitment to being a net security provider in the IOR.
• The outcome of the summit is expected to lead to new agreements and frameworks for enhanced coordination and collective response to maritime threats.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
India and its neighbourhood- relations.
keyTerms: SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region)
prelimsPoints: India hosted a summit on maritime security in the IOR.
SAGAR vision is central to India's regional engagement.
Challenges discussed include piracy, terrorism, illegal fishing.
Aims for joint patrols and intelligence sharing.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of the Indian Ocean Region for India and the various maritime security challenges it faces.
Discuss India's role as a net security provider in the IOR and the significance of regional cooperation frameworks like SAGAR.
Examine the impact of non-traditional maritime threats on regional stability and trade, and suggest comprehensive strategies to address them.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships patrolling in the Indian Ocean.
articles.trivia: The Indian Ocean is the third-largest of the world's five oceans.

articles.headline: Progress Report on India's Net-Zero Targets Highlights Accelerated Renewable Energy Capacity Addition
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy released a progress report showcasing significant strides in India's journey towards its net-zero emissions target by 2070.
• The report highlighted an accelerated pace of renewable energy capacity addition, particularly in solar and wind power projects.
• It detailed policy interventions, financial incentives, and infrastructure development supporting the transition away from fossil fuels.
• Challenges identified include grid integration of intermittent renewables, land acquisition, and ensuring a just transition for coal-dependent regions.
• India reiterated its commitment to achieving its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement, emphasizing energy security and sustainable growth.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India aims for net-zero emissions by 2070.
Significant increase in solar and wind power capacity.
NDCs are commitments under the Paris Agreement.
Challenges include grid integration and land acquisition.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's progress towards its net-zero emissions target, focusing on the role of renewable energy and associated policy measures.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in accelerating the transition to a low-carbon economy, considering energy security and economic development.
Examine the concept of 'just transition' in the context of India's energy shift and its implications for communities dependent on fossil fuel industries.
articles.imageDescription: Solar panels stretching into the horizon with wind turbines in the background.
articles.trivia: India's installed renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) is the 4th largest in the world.

articles.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous Development of Advanced Medical Devices Announced
articles.explainer:
• The Department of Biotechnology announced significant breakthroughs in the indigenous development and manufacturing of advanced medical devices.
• Researchers and startups have successfully developed prototypes for affordable diagnostic tools, surgical robots, and AI-powered health monitoring systems.
• This initiative aims to reduce India's reliance on imported medical technology, enhance healthcare accessibility, and boost the 'Make in India' campaign in the health sector.
• Government support includes funding for R&D, fast-tracking regulatory approvals, and establishing dedicated medical device parks.
• The advancements are expected to revolutionize healthcare delivery, making advanced medical care more affordable and available across the country.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: Breakthroughs in indigenous medical device development announced.
Includes diagnostic tools, surgical robots, AI health systems.
Aims to reduce import reliance and boost 'Make in India'.
Government provides R&D funding and regulatory support.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of indigenous development of medical devices for India's healthcare sector and its economic implications.
Analyze the challenges faced by India in becoming self-reliant in medical technology and the role of government policies in overcoming these challenges.
Examine how technological advancements like AI and robotics can transform healthcare delivery and accessibility in India.
articles.imageDescription: Close-up of a sophisticated medical device with a blurred laboratory background.
articles.trivia: The Indian medical devices market is projected to reach $50 billion by 2030.

articles.headline: Government Launches National Campaign for Mental Health Awareness Among Youth
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, in collaboration with the Ministry of Health, launched a nationwide campaign to raise mental health awareness among youth.
• The campaign focuses on destigmatizing mental health issues, promoting early intervention, and providing accessible support services for young people.
• It includes digital outreach programs, school-based mental health modules, and training for counselors and educators.
• The initiative acknowledges the increasing prevalence of mental health challenges among adolescents and young adults, exacerbated by modern lifestyles and academic pressures.
• The program aims to foster a supportive environment where youth can openly discuss mental health concerns and seek timely help.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
articles.source: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
prelimsPoints: National campaign targets mental health awareness among youth.
Focus on destigmatization and early intervention.
Includes digital programs and school-based modules.
Addresses increasing mental health challenges in young people.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing concern of mental health issues among Indian youth and its socio-economic consequences.
Analyze the effectiveness of public awareness campaigns and school-based interventions in addressing mental health challenges and destigmatizing mental illness.
Suggest policy measures and community-level initiatives to strengthen mental healthcare infrastructure and ensure accessible support for young people.
articles.imageDescription: Group of young people engaged in a supportive group discussion, symbolizing mental health awareness.
articles.trivia: According to WHO, one in seven Indians aged 15-24 years feels depressed or has other mental health issues.

highlights: PM GatiShakti 2.0 launched with a strong emphasis on green infrastructure and digital integration for logistics efficiency.
Supreme Court delivers landmark ruling clarifying parliamentary privileges in relation to fundamental rights, strengthening judicial review.
India advocates for Global South priorities at UNGA, calling for equitable climate finance and multilateral institution reforms.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee maintains status quo on repo rate, citing inflation concerns and global economic uncertainties.
ISRO successfully tests next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle technology, marking a significant step towards reducing space access costs.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the PM GatiShakti National Master Plan, consider the following statements:
1. It was launched in October 2021 to integrate infrastructure projects across 16 ministries.
2. The newly announced GatiShakti 2.0 specifically focuses on promoting green infrastructure and digital technologies.
3. One of its primary objectives is to reduce logistics costs in India to global benchmarks.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. PM GatiShakti was indeed launched in October 2021 to integrate 16 ministries for infrastructure development. GatiShakti 2.0 is an advanced phase focusing on green infrastructure and digital integration. A key objective of the plan is to reduce India's logistics costs to be competitive globally.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution deals with the powers, privileges, and immunities of the Houses of Parliament and of the members and committees thereof?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Article 105 of the Indian Constitution specifically deals with the powers, privileges, etc., of the Houses of Parliament and of the members and committees thereof. Article 19 relates to the protection of certain rights regarding freedom of speech, etc. Article 122 relates to courts not to inquire into proceedings of Parliament. Article 142 deals with the enforcement of decrees and orders of the Supreme Court.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs):
1. NCDs are a leading cause of morbidity and mortality globally, including in India.
2. Lifestyle modifications and early detection are crucial for the management and prevention of NCDs.
3. The National Health Mission (NHM) primarily focuses on communicable diseases and maternal and child health, with limited scope for NCDs.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. NCDs are indeed a major health burden, and lifestyle changes and early detection are vital. Statement 3 is incorrect. While NHM traditionally focused on communicable diseases and maternal & child health, its scope has expanded significantly to include NCDs, with dedicated programs for their prevention and control, as highlighted by the new initiative discussed.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's cyber security framework, consider the following statements:
1. The National Cyber Security Policy of India was introduced in 2013.
2. CERT-In is the nodal agency responsible for coordinating cyber security efforts in India.
3. The current review of the framework focuses solely on protecting government networks from cyberattacks.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. The National Cyber Security Policy was indeed introduced in 2013, and CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national nodal agency for cyber security incidents. Statement 3 is incorrect; the review of the framework is comprehensive, addressing threats to critical infrastructure, financial institutions, and the broader digital ecosystem, not just government networks.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in India is/are correct?
1. Article 44 of the Constitution mandates the state to endeavor to secure a UCC for the citizens.
2. Goa is the only Indian state that currently has a Uniform Civil Code.
3. The UCC aims to replace diverse personal laws governing marriage, divorce, inheritance, and adoption with a common set of laws.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. Article 44 is indeed a Directive Principle of State Policy calling for a UCC. Goa is currently the only Indian state with a UCC, inherited from its Portuguese colonial past. The primary objective of the UCC is to unify personal laws across all religious communities.

mainsMocks.question: The PM GatiShakti National Master Plan, now in its 2.0 phase, aims for integrated infrastructure development. Critically analyze its potential to transform India's logistics sector and accelerate economic growth, while also addressing the challenges of incorporating 'green' components.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.; Investment Models; Environmental Impact Assessment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce PM GatiShakti 2.0 and its objectives. Discuss its potential benefits for logistics efficiency, cost reduction, and economic growth (e.g., faster project execution, better multi-modal connectivity). Elaborate on the 'green' components (e.g., green highways, EV infra, renewable energy integration) and their contribution to sustainable development. Conclude by identifying key implementation challenges, such as inter-ministerial coordination, land acquisition, funding, and technological integration.

mainsMocks.question: The recent Supreme Court ruling on parliamentary privilege versus fundamental rights underscores the delicate balance between legislative autonomy and individual liberties. Discuss the constitutional provisions governing parliamentary privileges and critically examine how judicial interventions have shaped their interpretation in India's democratic framework.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by explaining the concept of parliamentary privilege and its constitutional basis (Article 105). Discuss the importance of these privileges for the effective functioning of the legislature. Then, delve into the conflict with fundamental rights, particularly freedom of speech (Article 19), and how the Supreme Court has historically intervened to define the limits of these privileges. Analyze the implications of the recent ruling for media freedom and democratic accountability, concluding on the necessity of a balanced approach.

mainsMocks.question: India's push for Global South leadership at the UN General Assembly reflects its evolving foreign policy priorities. Analyze India's strategic vision for the Global South, particularly concerning equitable climate finance and multilateral institution reforms. What challenges does India face in consolidating this leadership role?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Important International institutions, agencies and fora—their structure, mandate; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's growing assertiveness as a voice for the Global South and its significance at UNGA. Discuss India's vision, including its calls for equitable climate finance, technology transfer, and reform of multilateral institutions (e.g., UN Security Council, Bretton Woods institutions). Highlight India's own initiatives (e.g., ISA, CDRI). Conclude by discussing the challenges India faces, such as balancing its own national interests with those of the broader Global South, managing relations with major powers, and overcoming internal divergences within the Global South.

quickBytes.text: PM GatiShakti 2.0 emphasizes green infrastructure for sustainable logistics development.
Article 105 of the Indian Constitution deals with parliamentary privileges.
India advocated for equitable climate finance at the UN General Assembly.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) maintained the repo rate, citing inflation concerns.
ISRO successfully tested next-gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology.
The new National Policy on Sustainable Urban Waste Management promotes a circular economy.
National Health Mission's new initiative targets Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in urban areas.
India and Japan held talks to enhance defence cooperation and bilateral trade.
A parliamentary committee is discussing the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) with stakeholders.
India's Manufacturing PMI hit a record high, indicating robust economic expansion.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Delivers Landmark Ruling on Parliamentary Privilege vs. Fundamental Rights
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a crucial judgment clarifying the scope of parliamentary privileges in relation to the fundamental rights of citizens, particularly freedom of speech and expression.
• The ruling addressed a long-standing debate on the balance between safeguarding legislative proceedings and upholding individual liberties.
• The court emphasized that while parliamentary privileges are essential for the effective functioning of the legislature, they are not absolute and must be exercised in consonance with constitutional provisions.
• The judgment is expected to set a precedent for future cases involving conflicts between legislative authority and fundamental rights, impacting media reporting and public discourse.
• Legal experts highlight the ruling's importance in strengthening democratic accountability and ensuring that legislative actions remain subject to judicial review within defined parameters.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Article 105 deals with powers, privileges, etc., of the Houses of Parliament and of the members and committees thereof.
Article 19 guarantees freedom of speech and expression.
The ruling seeks to balance legislative autonomy with individual rights.
Parliamentary privileges are not codified in their entirety.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the delicate balance between parliamentary privileges and fundamental rights in a democratic setup, with reference to the recent Supreme Court judgment.
Discuss the implications of this ruling on the freedom of press and public discourse regarding legislative functioning.
Examine the historical context and evolution of parliamentary privileges in India and how judicial interventions have shaped their interpretation.
GS2.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building.
GS2.trivia: The concept of parliamentary privilege is largely derived from British parliamentary traditions and is enshrined in the Indian Constitution.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Powers, Privileges and Immunities of Parliament and its Members

GS2.headline: India Champions Global South Priorities at UN General Assembly, Calls for Equitable Climate Finance
GS2.explainer:
• India's External Affairs Minister addressed the United Nations General Assembly (UNGA), reiterating India's commitment to championing the voice of the Global South.
• The address focused on critical issues such as equitable access to climate finance, reform of multilateral institutions, and sustainable development goals (SDGs).
• India called for developed nations to fulfill their climate finance commitments and stressed the need for technology transfer to developing countries.
• The minister highlighted India's own contributions to global initiatives, including renewable energy projects and disaster resilience infrastructure, as a model for South-South cooperation.
• The statement underscored India's growing role as a leading advocate for a more inclusive and representative global order.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora—their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: UNGA is the main deliberative, policymaking and representative organ of the UN.
India is a strong advocate for Global South issues.

Climate finance refers to local, national or transnational financing—drawn from public, private and alternative sources of financing—that seeks to support mitigation and adaptation actions that will address climate change.
SDGs were adopted by all UN member states in 2015.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's evolving role as a leader of the Global South and its implications for international relations and multilateral institution reforms.
Analyze the challenges in achieving equitable climate finance and technology transfer, and critically evaluate India's proposals at the UNGA.
Examine how India's foreign policy prioritizes sustainable development and addresses global challenges like climate change and food security.
GS2.imageDescription: Image of the UN General Assembly hall with country delegates.
GS2.trivia: India has been a member of the United Nations since its founding in 1945.

GS2.headline: New National Health Mission Initiative Targets Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in Urban Areas
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched a new initiative under the National Health Mission (NHM) specifically designed to combat Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in urban settings.
• The program focuses on early detection, screening, and management of common NCDs like diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular diseases, and certain cancers.
• It emphasizes lifestyle modifications, public awareness campaigns, and strengthening primary healthcare infrastructure in urban areas to deliver comprehensive NCD care.
• Digital health solutions and community health workers will play a crucial role in reaching vulnerable populations and ensuring continuity of care.
• This initiative is a response to the growing burden of NCDs, which are a leading cause of morbidity and mortality in India, particularly in urban centers.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: NCDs include diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular diseases, and cancers.
The initiative is under the National Health Mission (NHM).
Focuses on early detection, screening, and management.
Aims to strengthen urban primary healthcare infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rising burden of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in India, especially in urban areas, and its socio-economic implications.
Discuss the strategies and challenges involved in implementing a comprehensive NCD control program under the National Health Mission.
Suggest innovative approaches and public-private partnerships that can enhance the effectiveness of NCD prevention and management in India.
GS2.imageDescription: Infographic showing common NCDs and preventive measures.
GS2.trivia: NCDs account for over 60% of all deaths in India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government Policies and Interventions for Health Sector

GS2.headline: India and Japan Hold High-Level Talks on Enhancing Defence Cooperation and Bilateral Trade
GS2.explainer:
• India and Japan conducted their annual strategic dialogue, focusing on strengthening defence cooperation, regional security, and boosting bilateral trade and investment.
• Discussions included joint military exercises, technology transfer in defence manufacturing, and cooperation in emerging security domains like cyber and space.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and discussed strategies to counter challenges to regional stability.
• Economic agenda covered diversification of supply chains, green energy investments, and collaboration in critical and emerging technologies.
• The talks underscored the growing convergence of strategic interests between India and Japan, key partners in the Quad framework.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan are strategic partners and members of the Quad.
Discussions covered defence, trade, and regional security.
Both nations advocate for a free and open Indo-Pacific.
Focus on critical and emerging technologies and green energy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-Japan strategic partnership in shaping the geopolitical landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how enhanced defence cooperation and economic collaboration between India and Japan contribute to their respective national interests and regional stability.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India and Japan in diversifying supply chains and fostering innovation in critical technologies.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed during a bilateral meeting.
GS2.trivia: India and Japan established a 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership' in 2014.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Committee Discusses Uniform Civil Code (UCC) with Legal Experts and Stakeholders
GS2.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee held extensive consultations on the Uniform Civil Code (UCC), inviting views from various legal experts, religious organizations, and civil society groups.
• The discussions focused on the constitutional aspects of UCC, its potential impact on personal laws, and the challenges in achieving consensus across diverse communities.
• Committee members explored different models for UCC implementation, including a phased approach or specific legislative reforms rather than a wholesale overhaul of personal laws.
• Stakeholders presented arguments both for and against the UCC, highlighting concerns about cultural diversity and gender justice implications.
• The committee's report is expected to provide recommendations on the feasibility and framework for a potential Uniform Civil Code in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Article 44 of the Indian Constitution directs the state to endeavor to secure a UCC.
UCC aims to replace diverse personal laws with a common set of laws governing marriage, divorce, inheritance, etc.
Parliamentary committees play a crucial role in legislative scrutiny.
Goa is the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code.
mainsPoints: Examine the constitutional mandate and socio-political implications of implementing a Uniform Civil Code in India.
Discuss the arguments for and against the UCC, considering issues of cultural diversity, gender justice, and religious freedom.
Analyze the challenges in building consensus for a UCC and suggest potential approaches for its phased implementation, drawing lessons from international experiences.
GS2.imageDescription: Image of the Indian Parliament building.
GS2.trivia: The Supreme Court has, on several occasions, urged the government to implement a Uniform Civil Code.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Report Highlights Progress in Skill Development
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a progress report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, focusing on skill development initiatives.
• The report highlighted significant strides in integrating vocational education into mainstream schooling and higher education curricula.
• It showcased the establishment of new skill hubs, industry partnerships for internships, and the introduction of credit-based vocational courses.
• The policy aims to equip students with future-ready skills, enhance employability, and bridge the skill gap in various sectors.
• Challenges remain in scaling vocational training infrastructure and ensuring equitable access across rural and urban areas.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 seeks to integrate vocational education.
Progress reported in establishing skill hubs and industry partnerships.
Aims to equip students with future-ready skills.
Challenges include scaling infrastructure and equitable access.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the progress and challenges in implementing the skill development objectives of the National Education Policy 2020.
Discuss how vocational education can be effectively integrated into India's mainstream education system to enhance youth employability and address the skill gap.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to quality skill development programs, particularly for students in rural and marginalized communities.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaged in vocational training activities.
GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history after those of 1968 and 1986.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government Policies and Interventions for Education Sector

GS2.headline: Election Commission Explores Digital Voting Pilot Projects and Other Electoral Reforms
GS2.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) announced its intention to explore pilot projects for digital voting, particularly for absentee voters and specific demographics.
• The discussions on electoral reforms also included proposals for greater transparency in campaign financing, regulation of social media during elections, and strengthening the Model Code of Conduct.
• ECI emphasized ensuring the security, integrity, and verifiability of any digital voting system to maintain public trust.
• The move is aimed at enhancing voter participation and making the electoral process more accessible and efficient.
• Stakeholder consultations are underway to gather feedback from political parties, technology experts, and civil society organizations on these proposed reforms.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
prelimsPoints: ECI exploring digital voting pilot projects.
Aims to enhance voter participation and accessibility.
Reforms also include campaign finance and social media regulation.
Integrity and security of digital voting are key concerns.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential benefits and challenges of introducing digital voting in India, particularly for absentee voters and NRIs.
Analyze the significance of electoral reforms in strengthening democratic processes and ensuring free and fair elections in India.
Examine the role of the Election Commission of India in upholding the integrity of elections and adapting to new technological and social challenges.
GS2.imageDescription: Symbolic image of a ballot box with digital elements, representing electoral modernization.
GS2.trivia: The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body established under Article 324 of the Constitution.

GS2.headline: India Hosts Regional Summit on Enhancing Maritime Security Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted a high-level regional summit focused on strengthening maritime security cooperation among littoral states in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR).
• The summit addressed challenges such as piracy, maritime terrorism, illegal fishing, and drug trafficking, emphasizing a collaborative approach.
• Discussions included proposals for joint patrols, intelligence sharing mechanisms, and capacity building for coastal nations.
• India reiterated its vision for SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) and its commitment to being a net security provider in the IOR.
• The outcome of the summit is expected to lead to new agreements and frameworks for enhanced coordination and collective response to maritime threats.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
India and its neighbourhood- relations.
keyTerms: SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region)
prelimsPoints: India hosted a summit on maritime security in the IOR.
SAGAR vision is central to India's regional engagement.
Challenges discussed include piracy, terrorism, illegal fishing.
Aims for joint patrols and intelligence sharing.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of the Indian Ocean Region for India and the various maritime security challenges it faces.
Discuss India's role as a net security provider in the IOR and the significance of regional cooperation frameworks like SAGAR.
Examine the impact of non-traditional maritime threats on regional stability and trade, and suggest comprehensive strategies to address them.
GS2.imageDescription: Naval ships patrolling in the Indian Ocean.
GS2.trivia: The Indian Ocean is the third-largest of the world's five oceans.

GS2.headline: Government Launches National Campaign for Mental Health Awareness Among Youth
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, in collaboration with the Ministry of Health, launched a nationwide campaign to raise mental health awareness among youth.
• The campaign focuses on destigmatizing mental health issues, promoting early intervention, and providing accessible support services for young people.
• It includes digital outreach programs, school-based mental health modules, and training for counselors and educators.
• The initiative acknowledges the increasing prevalence of mental health challenges among adolescents and young adults, exacerbated by modern lifestyles and academic pressures.
• The program aims to foster a supportive environment where youth can openly discuss mental health concerns and seek timely help.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
GS2.source: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
prelimsPoints: National campaign targets mental health awareness among youth.
Focus on destigmatization and early intervention.
Includes digital programs and school-based modules.
Addresses increasing mental health challenges in young people.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing concern of mental health issues among Indian youth and its socio-economic consequences.
Analyze the effectiveness of public awareness campaigns and school-based interventions in addressing mental health challenges and destigmatizing mental illness.
Suggest policy measures and community-level initiatives to strengthen mental healthcare infrastructure and ensure accessible support for young people.
GS2.imageDescription: Group of young people engaged in a supportive group discussion, symbolizing mental health awareness.
GS2.trivia: According to WHO, one in seven Indians aged 15-24 years feels depressed or has other mental health issues.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils PM GatiShakti 2.0 with Focus on Green Infrastructure and Logistics Efficiency
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government announced an advanced phase of the PM GatiShakti National Master Plan, termed GatiShakti 2.0, emphasizing sustainable development and green infrastructure.
• This new phase aims to further integrate planning and execution across 16 ministries for multi-modal connectivity and logistics efficiency, incorporating environmental sustainability.
• Key focus areas include developing green highways, promoting electric vehicle charging infrastructure along corridors, and integrating renewable energy projects into logistics hubs.
• The initiative seeks to leverage digital technologies like AI and machine learning for predictive analytics in project management and reduce carbon footprint in the logistics sector.
• Expected outcomes include significant cost reduction for businesses, faster project implementation, and enhanced environmental stewardship through sustainable practices.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment Models
Environmental Impact Assessment
prelimsPoints: PM GatiShakti launched in October 2021.
It integrates 16 ministries for infrastructure development.
GatiShakti 2.0 focuses on green infrastructure and digital integration.
Aims to reduce logistics costs and enhance efficiency.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of integrated infrastructure planning for India's economic growth and global competitiveness.
Analyze how the 'green' component of GatiShakti 2.0 contributes to India's sustainable development goals and climate commitments.
Evaluate the potential challenges and opportunities in implementing a large-scale, digitally-driven infrastructure project like GatiShakti 2.0.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration depicting integrated infrastructure projects like roads, railways, and ports with green energy elements.
GS3.trivia: PM GatiShakti aims to cut logistics costs from 13-14% of GDP to 8% by 2030, aligning with global benchmarks.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Concerns and Global Volatility
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly meeting, deciding to maintain the repo rate at its current level.
• The decision was influenced by persistent concerns over elevated core inflation and the need to ensure price stability, despite moderating headline inflation.
• The MPC also highlighted global economic uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions and volatile commodity prices, as key factors impacting India's economic outlook.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to supporting economic growth while remaining vigilant against inflationary pressures, maintaining an 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• Analysts suggest that future rate decisions will heavily depend on the trajectory of monsoon, global oil prices, and government fiscal measures.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
MPC consists of six members (3 from RBI, 3 appointed by Government).
Primary objective of MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth in the current global and domestic economic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflationary pressures in India.
Examine the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance of the RBI and its implications for credit growth and investment in the Indian economy.
GS3.imageDescription: Image of the Reserve Bank of India headquarters in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: New National Policy on Sustainable Urban Waste Management Launched to Boost Circular Economy
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched a comprehensive National Policy on Sustainable Urban Waste Management, aiming for a circular economy approach.
• The policy focuses on source segregation, waste-to-wealth initiatives, promoting advanced recycling technologies, and reducing landfill dependency.
• It mandates greater involvement of urban local bodies (ULBs) and private sector players in waste collection, processing, and disposal, with emphasis on Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR).
• Capacity building for ULB officials and awareness campaigns for citizens on waste reduction and segregation are integral parts of the new framework.
• The policy is aligned with India's Swachh Bharat Mission and aims to achieve significant improvements in urban sanitation and environmental quality by 2030.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
GS3.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: The policy aims to reduce landfill dependency and promote recycling.
It emphasizes source segregation of waste.
Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is a key component.
Aligned with Swachh Bharat Mission goals.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the potential of a circular economy approach in addressing India's urban waste management challenges.
Discuss the role of technology and public-private partnerships in implementing the new National Policy on Sustainable Urban Waste Management.
Analyze the socio-economic and environmental benefits of effective waste management and the challenges in achieving behavioral change among citizens.
GS3.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating the waste management cycle from segregation to recycling and waste-to-energy.
GS3.trivia: India generates over 1.5 lakh metric tonnes of municipal solid waste every day.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful test of its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology demonstrator.
• This critical test involved controlled landing and precise navigation maneuvers, simulating future operational flights.
• The RLV program aims to significantly reduce the cost of space access by enabling repeated use of launch vehicles, a key goal for future space missions.
• The technology is expected to pave the way for India's own space shuttle-like capabilities, enhancing its competitive edge in the global space market.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing fully reusable rockets, which are crucial for ambitious future missions like human spaceflight and satellite mega-constellations.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV program aims to reduce space launch costs.
The test involved controlled landing and navigation.
RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) is part of this program.
India is developing its own reusable rocket technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its global competitiveness.
Analyze the economic and scientific benefits of reducing the cost of space access through technologies like RLV.
Examine the challenges and future prospects for India in developing fully reusable rocket systems and their impact on future space missions.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle prototype during re-entry.
GS3.trivia: ISRO first successfully tested the RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) in 2016, performing an atmospheric re-entry experiment.

GS3.headline: Government Initiates Comprehensive Review of India's Cyber Security Framework Amidst Rising Threats
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has initiated a comprehensive review of India's existing cyber security framework.
• The move comes in response to a surge in sophisticated cyberattacks targeting critical infrastructure, government networks, and financial institutions.
• The review aims to strengthen existing laws, update national strategies, and enhance coordination among various agencies, including CERT-In.
• Emphasis will be placed on developing indigenous cyber security capabilities, fostering public-private partnerships, and building a skilled workforce.
• The updated framework is expected to address emerging threats like AI-driven cyberattacks and supply chain vulnerabilities, ensuring a robust digital ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
prelimsPoints: MeitY is reviewing India's cyber security framework.
CERT-In is the national agency for responding to cyber security incidents.
The review addresses sophisticated cyber threats and critical infrastructure protection.
Focus on indigenous capabilities and public-private partnerships.
mainsPoints: Examine the evolving landscape of cyber threats in India and the challenges in protecting critical national infrastructure.
Discuss the key components of a robust national cyber security strategy and how the proposed review aims to strengthen India's digital defenses.
Analyze the role of international cooperation and indigenous capability building in enhancing India's cyber security posture.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract image representing cyber security with digital locks and network lines.
GS3.trivia: The National Cyber Security Policy of India was introduced in 2013.

GS3.headline: Government Boosts Agricultural Exports and Value Chain Development with New Policy Measures
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare announced new policy measures aimed at significantly boosting India's agricultural exports and strengthening value chains.
• The initiatives include enhanced support for farmer producer organizations (FPOs), investment in post-harvest infrastructure, and promotion of 'Brand India' for agricultural products.
• Focus areas are high-value agricultural commodities, organic products, and processed foods, targeting new international markets.
• The policy emphasizes technology adoption, quality control, and adherence to international phytosanitary standards to enhance competitiveness.
• This move is expected to increase farmers' income, reduce post-harvest losses, and position India as a major global agricultural exporter.
