gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
GS3.source: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
prelimsPoints: New policies aim to boost agricultural exports.
Support for FPOs and post-harvest infrastructure are key.
Focus on high-value and organic agricultural products.
Aims to increase farmer income and reduce losses.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of India's agricultural sector to become a global export powerhouse and the policy interventions required to achieve this.
Analyze how strengthening agricultural value chains and promoting FPOs can enhance farmer income and reduce post-harvest losses.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in ensuring adherence to international quality and phytosanitary standards for Indian agricultural exports.
GS3.imageDescription: Image of diverse Indian agricultural produce ready for export.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the world's largest producers of several agricultural commodities, including milk, pulses, and spices.

GS3.headline: New Initiatives Launched for Conservation of Endangered Species and Their Habitats
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change unveiled a series of new initiatives aimed at strengthening the conservation efforts for several endangered species across India.
• The focus is on critical habitats, establishing more eco-sensitive zones, and implementing community-based conservation models.
• Projects include enhanced protection for specific species like the Great Indian Bustard, Snow Leopard, and various freshwater turtle species.
• The initiatives incorporate advanced technologies like drone surveillance, genetic monitoring, and satellite tracking for effective wildlife management.
• These efforts align with India's commitments under the Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD) and aim to protect the nation's rich biodiversity.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Initiatives target endangered species like Great Indian Bustard, Snow Leopard.
Focus on critical habitats and eco-sensitive zones.
Utilizes drone surveillance and genetic monitoring.
Aligned with India's CBD commitments.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major threats to biodiversity in India and evaluate the effectiveness of current conservation strategies.
Analyze the role of community participation and technological advancements in strengthening wildlife protection and habitat conservation.
Examine India's international commitments for biodiversity conservation and how national initiatives contribute to achieving these goals.
GS3.imageDescription: Picture of a Great Indian Bustard in its natural habitat.
GS3.trivia: India is home to 4 of the 36 global biodiversity hotspots.

GS3.headline: Government Announces New Policy to Foster Deep-Tech Startups and Innovation Ecosystem
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Science & Technology unveiled a new national policy aimed at accelerating the growth of deep-tech startups in India.
• The policy provides financial incentives, easier access to research infrastructure, and streamlined regulatory processes for startups working on cutting-edge technologies.
• Focus areas include Artificial Intelligence (AI), Quantum Computing, Biotechnology, Advanced Materials, and Space Technology.
• It emphasizes collaboration between academia, industry, and government to create a robust innovation ecosystem and attract venture capital.
• The initiative seeks to position India as a global hub for deep-tech innovation, driving economic growth and creating high-skilled jobs.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: New policy targets deep-tech startups.
Provides financial incentives and infrastructure access.
Focuses on AI, Quantum Computing, Biotechnology, etc.
Aims to foster collaboration between academia, industry, government.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of deep-tech startups for India's economic growth, national security, and global competitiveness.
Analyze the challenges faced by deep-tech startups in India and how the new policy aims to address these issues.
Examine the role of government, academia, and private sector in building a thriving deep-tech innovation ecosystem in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract image representing advanced technology and innovation with glowing circuits.
GS3.trivia: India ranks third globally in terms of the number of startups, with over 100 unicorns.

GS3.headline: India's Manufacturing PMI Hits Record High, Signaling Robust Economic Recovery
GS3.explainer:
• India's Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) reached a record high in September, indicating strong expansion in the manufacturing sector.
• The surge was driven by a robust increase in new orders, higher output, and improved business confidence, reflecting healthy domestic demand.
• Employment in the manufacturing sector also saw a notable rise, suggesting job creation and positive sentiment among businesses.
• Despite some input cost pressures, firms remained optimistic about future production, with export orders also contributing to the growth.
• This sustained growth in manufacturing is a key indicator of India's broader economic recovery and resilience amidst global headwinds.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Industrial policy and its effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: PMI is an indicator of economic trends in the manufacturing and service sectors.
A PMI reading above 50 indicates expansion.
Record high PMI signifies strong growth in manufacturing.
Driven by new orders, output, and employment growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the robust growth in India's manufacturing PMI and its implications for the overall economic recovery.
Discuss the role of government policies, such as 'Make in India' and PLI schemes, in boosting the manufacturing sector.
Examine the challenges that persist for India's manufacturing sector despite positive indicators, such as global supply chain disruptions and competition.
GS3.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating an upward trend in economic indicators with factory silhouettes.
GS3.trivia: The Services PMI and Composite PMI are other key indicators tracking economic activity.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Growth and Development of Indian Economy

GS3.headline: Progress Report on India's Net-Zero Targets Highlights Accelerated Renewable Energy Capacity Addition
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy released a progress report showcasing significant strides in India's journey towards its net-zero emissions target by 2070.
• The report highlighted an accelerated pace of renewable energy capacity addition, particularly in solar and wind power projects.
• It detailed policy interventions, financial incentives, and infrastructure development supporting the transition away from fossil fuels.
• Challenges identified include grid integration of intermittent renewables, land acquisition, and ensuring a just transition for coal-dependent regions.
• India reiterated its commitment to achieving its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement, emphasizing energy security and sustainable growth.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India aims for net-zero emissions by 2070.
Significant increase in solar and wind power capacity.
NDCs are commitments under the Paris Agreement.
Challenges include grid integration and land acquisition.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's progress towards its net-zero emissions target, focusing on the role of renewable energy and associated policy measures.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in accelerating the transition to a low-carbon economy, considering energy security and economic development.
Examine the concept of 'just transition' in the context of India's energy shift and its implications for communities dependent on fossil fuel industries.
GS3.imageDescription: Solar panels stretching into the horizon with wind turbines in the background.
GS3.trivia: India's installed renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) is the 4th largest in the world.

GS3.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous Development of Advanced Medical Devices Announced
GS3.explainer:
• The Department of Biotechnology announced significant breakthroughs in the indigenous development and manufacturing of advanced medical devices.
• Researchers and startups have successfully developed prototypes for affordable diagnostic tools, surgical robots, and AI-powered health monitoring systems.
• This initiative aims to reduce India's reliance on imported medical technology, enhance healthcare accessibility, and boost the 'Make in India' campaign in the health sector.
• Government support includes funding for R&D, fast-tracking regulatory approvals, and establishing dedicated medical device parks.
• The advancements are expected to revolutionize healthcare delivery, making advanced medical care more affordable and available across the country.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: Breakthroughs in indigenous medical device development announced.
Includes diagnostic tools, surgical robots, AI health systems.
Aims to reduce import reliance and boost 'Make in India'.
Government provides R&D funding and regulatory support.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of indigenous development of medical devices for India's healthcare sector and its economic implications.
Analyze the challenges faced by India in becoming self-reliant in medical technology and the role of government policies in overcoming these challenges.
Examine how technological advancements like AI and robotics can transform healthcare delivery and accessibility in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Close-up of a sophisticated medical device with a blurred laboratory background.
GS3.trivia: The Indian medical devices market is projected to reach $50 billion by 2030.
